seo.title: AI for Personal Injury Firms — Depos + Records | Dodonai
seo.description: PI cases live in depositions and medical records. Dodonai turns both into prep-ready summaries and chronologies in minutes.

hero.headline: AI for Personal Injury Firms: Depositions and Medical Records in Minutes
hero.subheadline: Every PI case is built on two stacks of documents — deposition transcripts and medical records. Dodonai turns both into case-ready summaries and chronologies with citations, so case prep that took associate-weeks happens in an afternoon.

sections.heading: The Two Workflows That Define PI Case Prep
paragraphs: A personal injury firm's economics depend on two things: how fast a case moves from intake to demand, and how strong the file is when it gets there. Both come down to document work — turning deposition transcripts into cross-examination outlines, and turning thousands of pages of medical records into a clean chronology that supports the damages model.

Done manually, this is associate and paralegal time measured in weeks per case. Outsourced to a summary service, it's a 3–7 business day turnaround at $3–$10 per page. With AI built for legal workflow, it's an afternoon at a fraction of the cost — and the output is consistent across every case in the firm.

sections.heading: What PI Firms Use Dodonai For

cards.title: Deposition Summaries with Citations
cards.description: Upload the plaintiff, defendant, and expert deposition transcripts. Receive page-line summaries, narrative summaries, or issue-based summaries in roughly 3 minutes per 100 pages. Every point cites back to the testimony for instant verification.

cards.title: Medical Record Chronologies
cards.description: Process the full medical records production — ER visits, hospitalizations, specialist referrals, surgeries, PT sessions, medication changes, IME findings — into a date-ordered chronology with page citations. Surfaces gaps in treatment, prior conditions, and IME contradictions.

cards.title: Search Across the Whole Case File
cards.description: Load every transcript, expert report, and medical record into the case. Search by meaning, not just keywords. Find every place a defense expert mentioned causation, every reference to a preexisting condition, every inconsistency.

cards.title: Same-Week Demand Letter Inputs
cards.description: Medical chronology, treatment cost summary, and deposition highlights in the same week the records arrive. Cases move from intake to demand in a fraction of the standard timeline.

cards.title: HIPAA-Compliant by Default
cards.description: Medical records are protected health information. Dodonai is SOC 2 certified, HIPAA compliant, AES-256 encrypted, and signs BAAs. Your case data is never used to train AI models.

cards.title: Predictable Cost Per Case
cards.description: Subscription pricing — Lite $30/month for 200 pages, Basic $60/month for 1,000 pages, Pro $100/month for 5,000 pages. A typical PI case file runs $5–$30 to fully process, against hundreds of dollars per case for outsourced summary services.

sections.heading: Typical PI Case Flow with Dodonai

items.title: Upload the Case Documents
items.description: Drop in deposition transcripts, medical records, IME reports, and expert disclosures. PDF, Word, scanned files, and handwritten records all work. No preprocessing required.

items.title: Generate the Core Outputs
items.description: Run deposition summaries, build the medical chronology, and process the IME report. Each output completes in minutes and includes page citations to the source.

items.title: Search the Full Case File
items.description: Use semantic search across the whole case to find every mention of causation, prior conditions, treatment gaps, or specific medications. Search by meaning so phrasing differences don't hide relevant testimony.

items.title: Build the Demand Package
items.description: Compile the chronology, deposition highlights, treatment cost summary, and expert findings into a demand letter or settlement memo. Source citations carry through to the final document.

sections.heading: Beyond Personal Injury
paragraphs: PI firms often grow into adjacent practice areas — medical malpractice, workers' compensation, mass tort, product liability. Dodonai works the same way across all of them: same deposition summaries, same medical chronologies, same searchable case library. Firms that adopt Dodonai for PI usually expand it across the rest of the litigation practice within a few months because the workflow is the same.

faq.question: How does Dodonai compare to AI tools built specifically for personal injury firms?
faq.answer: PI-focused AI tools tend to bundle case management, intake, and document review into a single platform. Dodonai is focused on the document review piece — deposition summaries, medical chronologies, and case-file search — and integrates alongside whatever case management system you already use. The tradeoff: less bundling, more focus on output quality and turnaround speed for the documents that drive case value.

faq.question: Can Dodonai handle the volume of records on a complex PI case?
faq.answer: Yes. The Pro plan processes 5,000 pages per month at $100. A typical PI case file — full medical records, two or three depositions, IME report, expert disclosures — usually runs 800 to 2,500 pages. Enterprise plans are available for high-volume firms or mass tort docket work.

faq.question: Are the medical chronologies usable for demand letters?
faq.answer: Yes. The chronology is date-ordered with each entry citing the source record page, which makes it easy to pull directly into a demand letter and link evidence back to the underlying records. The chronology surfaces treatment gaps, prior conditions, and inconsistencies that affect case value.

faq.question: How does Dodonai protect protected health information?
faq.answer: SOC 2 certified, HIPAA compliant, AES-256 encryption from upload to deletion, and signed BAAs. Your data is never used to train AI models, and you control the retention schedule. Medical records workflow was a foundational use case, not an add-on.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to see if Dodonai fits our firm?
faq.answer: Start the free trial, upload one real deposition transcript and one set of medical records from a recent case, and compare the output to what your team produced manually. Most PI firms make a decision after running one or two cases through.
